sections.instruction: Questions 1-6: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 7-10: Who wants to do each of the activities below? A only Jacinta B only Lewis C both Jacinta and Lewis. Write the correct letter A, B or C next to questions 7-10.
sections.passage_or_context: Budget accommodation in Queenstown, New Zealand

sections.instruction: Questions 11-14: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 15-17: List THREE types of organizations for which CitiCyclist provides services. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 18-20: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: Jack says that in London these days many people
options: A see cycling as a foolish activity
B have on experience of cycling
C take too many risks when cycling

questions.content: If people want to cycle to school or work, CitiCyclist helps them by
options: A giving cycling lessons on the route they take.
B advising them on the safest route to choose.
C teaching them basic skills on quiet roads first.

questions.content: Jack works with some advanced cyclists who want to develop
options: C confidence in complex road systems.

questions.content: CitiCyclist supports the view that cyclists should
options: A have separate sections of the road from motor traffic.
B always wear protective clothing when cycling.
C know how to ride confidently on busy roads.

questions.content: What do Sharon and Xiao Li agree was the strongest aspect of their presentation? {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 31-33: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 34-40: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: The World Health Organisation says a healthy city must • have a {{BLANK}} and environment. • meet the {{BLANK}} of all its inhabitants. • provide easily accessible health services. • encourage ordinary people to take part in {{BLANK}}

questions.content: The World Health Organisation says a healthy city must have a {{BLANK}} and environment.
The World Health Organisation says a healthy city must meet the {{BLANK}} of all its inhabitants.
